mission.title: Install and Configure Redpanda
mission.description: Redpanda is a high-performance streaming data platform that is Kafka API compatible, designed for developers seeking a simpler and faster alternative to traditional streaming solutions. It eliminates the need for ZooKeeper and JVM, making it lightweight and easy to operate. Ideal for real-time data processing and analytics, Redpanda offers a robust ecosystem of connectors and tiered storage options.

steps.title: Install the Redpanda CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Redpanda command-line interface (CLI) which is essential for managing Redpanda clusters.

```bash

curl -1sLf 'https://dl.redpanda.com/nzc4ZYQK3WRGd9sy/redpanda/cfg/setup/bash.deb.sh' | sudo -E bash

sudo apt-get install redpanda

```

> This command sets up the repository for Debian/Ubuntu.
> This command installs the Redpanda CLI.

steps.title: Create a Redpanda cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Redpanda cluster on your Kubernetes environment with the specified version and node count.

```bash

kubectl create namespace redpanda

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/redpanda.yaml

```

> Create a namespace for Redpanda.
> Deploy Redpanda using the provided YAML configuration.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to access the Redpanda cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$HOME/.kube/config

kubectl config use-context <your-cluster-context>

```

> Set the kubeconfig environment variable.
> Replace <your-cluster-context> with your actual cluster context.

steps.title: Verify Redpanda nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Redpanda nodes to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redpanda

kubectl logs -l app=redpanda -n redpanda

```

> List all pods in the Redpanda namespace to verify their status.
> Check logs for any errors or issues.

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for your Redpanda cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Deploy autoscaler for Redpanda.
> Deploy monitoring tools for Redpanda.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Redpanda
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Redpanda deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete namespace redpanda

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/redpanda.yaml

```

> This command deletes the Redpanda namespace and all resources within it.
> Remove the Redpanda deployment.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Redpanda
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and check for any deprecated features. This step performs the upgrade and verifies the installation.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n redpanda -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/redpanda-data/redpanda/v25.3.10/deploy/redpanda.yaml

kubectl get pods -n redpanda

```

> Drain the nodes before upgrade.
> Apply the new version of the Redpanda deployment.
> Verify that all pods are running the new version.
commands: kubectl get pods -n redpanda -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs -I {} kubectl drain {} --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Redpanda service is not running or the kubeconfig is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Redpanda pods are running and check your kubeconfig settings.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Redpanda pod is failing to start, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs for errors and ensure sufficient resources are allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No nodes available for Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if there are no available nodes in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the node status and ensure they are ready.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient permissions to access Redpanda'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RBAC settings are not correctly configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the service account has the necessary permissions to access Redpanda resources.

versionNotes.changes: Improved performance metrics, added support for tiered storage, and enhanced Kafka compatibility.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy configuration flags related to storage.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.2.x, review the new storage configuration settings.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with all pods in the 'Running' state when everything is working.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
